summary:
Inhibition reduces aqueous humor formation due to altered bicarbonate levels; thus, decreasing IOP for managing acute angle-closure glaucoma effectively.
Acetazolamide inhibits carbonic anhydrase leading to reduced aqueous humor secretion via changes in hydrogen ion concentrations which lowers eye pressure suitable for treating angle-closure glaucoma patients. Summary:

Inhibiting carbonic anhydrase with acetazolamide decreases aqueous humor production by altering bicarbonate levels, thereby reducing intraocular pressure effective against angle-closure glaucoma. The primary mechanism relies on modulating pH-dependent processes within ocular tissues. 

Note: Adjusted sentence structure slightly but kept core information intact focusing only on essential details related directly back to the original requirements provided.
